          # Create a relative clause structure without repeating the subject
          # Extract predicate and object from second clause by removing subject
          sub1 = clauses[0].split()[0]  # First word of first clause
          sub2 = clauses[1].split()[0]  # First word of second clause
  
          # If subjects match, create relative clause without repeating subject
          if sub1.lower() == sub2.lower():
              # Get the rest of the second clause after the subject
              rest = clauses[1].replace(sub2, "", 1).strip()
              # Capitalize first letter of rest
              rest = rest[0].upper() + rest[1:]
              sentence = clauses[0] + ", which " + rest.lower() + "."
          else:
              sentence = clauses[0] + ", which " + clauses[1].lower() + "."

          # Build a more natural complex sentence with multiple clauses

with:
  # Combine clauses into a single complex sentence
      if len(clauses) == 1:
          return clauses[0].capitalize() + "."
  
      # Identify the main subject (usually the airport in first position)
      main_subject = clauses[0].split()[0] if clauses else ""
  
      # Build a flowing complex sentence by connecting related clauses
      result_parts = []
      result_parts.append(clauses[0])
  
      for i, clause in enumerate(clauses[1:], start=1):
          # Extract subject from clause (first word)
          clause_words = clause.split()
          clause_subject = clause_words[0] if clause_words else ""
  
          # Get the rest of the clause after the subject
          rest = clause.replace(clause_subject, "", 1).strip()
  
          # Determine connector based on relationship
          if clause_subject.lower() == main_subject.lower():
              # Same subject - use "which" to continue
              connector = ", which "
          elif clause_subject.lower() in clauses[0].lower():
              # Referenced entity from previous clause - use "where" or "which"
              connector = ", where " if "location" in clause.lower() or "country" in clause.lower()...
          else:
              # Different entity - use "and" with relative clause
              connector = ", and "
